2026-04-27 Reuters Japan FX outlook Iran ceasefire BOJ FOMC USD JPY

snapshot: Chicago Nikkei 225 futures were 60,045 yen, up 325 versus the Osaka close, leaving the cash market with room to test the 60,000 mark after Friday's 59,716.18 close.
U.S. semiconductor strength and Nasdaq gains are supportive, but higher oil and stalled U.S.-Iran talks can cap upside.
Brent touched $107.97 and USD/JPY was around the mid-159s. That helps resources but keeps import-cost pressure alive.
The BOJ meeting and FOMC are both this week, so post-open follow-through is likely to be more selective than the futures level alone suggests.
After-close catalysts are heavy in buybacks and guidance raises, making stock-specific checks more important than broad index chasing.

drivers.driver: U.S. semiconductor strength and higher futures point to a 60,000 test
drivers.why_today: Chicago Nikkei 225 futures were 325 yen above Osaka, and U.S. chip strength should support AI, semiconductor and robotics names at the open.

drivers.driver: Higher oil and USD/JPY in the mid-159s keep cost pressure alive
drivers.why_today: Brent moved into the $107 area and dollar/yen remains high. That favors resources and capital-action names over a simple all-in risk-on trade.

drivers.driver: Friday after-close buybacks and guidance raises provide the cleanest individual setups
drivers.why_today: NRI, FANUC and Kikkoman announced buybacks, while Techno Horizon, Harmonic and MIXI raised guidance. These are the names to verify first after the opening print.

headline_inventory.headline: Chicago Nikkei 225 futures were 60,045 yen, up 325 yen versus the Osaka close
As U.S.-Iran talks stalled, Brent touched $107.97, S&P 500 futures slipped 0.3%, and USD/JPY was around 159.53
Reuters' weekly FX outlook puts USD/JPY in a 157.50-161.50 range while markets watch the BOJ, FOMC and Iran situation
The April 27 equity outlook expects gains, with the Nikkei's 60,000 level in focus after stronger U.S. semiconductor shares
Techno Horizon raised ordinary-profit guidance by 67%, now targeting its first record profit in five years and a 10-yen dividend increase
NRI approved a buyback capped at 3.66% of shares outstanding and 70 billion yen
FANUC disclosed a buyback capped at 10 million shares and 50 billion yen, plus treasury-share cancellation
Harmonic Drive raised ordinary-profit guidance by 67%, helped by stronger robot and semiconductor-equipment demand
MIXI raised ordinary-profit guidance by 29%, reducing the expected profit decline materially
Kikkoman approved a buyback capped at 2.59% of shares outstanding and 30 billion yen

watchlist.name: Techno Horizon
watchlist.why_today: Techno Horizon raised ordinary-profit guidance by 67%, now targeting a five-year high, and lifted the dividend by 10 yen. It is one of the clearest after-close catalysts this morning.
watchpoints: Can the stock keep a strong bid after the open
Does the market reward both the guidance raise and the dividend increase
Does physical-AI and robotics buying continue alongside it
falsifiers: Profit-taking dominates immediately after a high open
The move turns into a small-cap theme spike only
watchlist.red_team: The catalyst is strong, but if the theme premium runs too fast, the stock can fade quickly later in the day.

watchlist.name: Nomura Research Institute
watchlist.why_today: NRI approved a buyback capped at 3.66% and 70 billion yen. As a liquid large-cap, the supply-improvement story is easy to validate at the open.
watchpoints: Can it hold the 6,000-yen area with real turnover
Does the market reward the 70 billion yen buyback cleanly
Does buying remain concentrated in NRI rather than the whole IT-services group
falsifiers: The buyback is treated as already priced in
watchlist.red_team: The catalyst is clear, but as a large-cap name the immediate range expansion can still be limited.

watchlist.name: FANUC
watchlist.why_today: FANUC guided to growth on solid robot and FA demand and paired that with a 50 billion yen buyback. It has both index relevance and a robotics angle.
watchpoints: Does buying continue after the open alongside U.S. semiconductor strength
Does the market reward both the buyback and the earnings outlook
Does robotics and FA demand remain a leadership theme
falsifiers: The outlook is treated as broadly in line with consensus
U.S. tech futures fade and pressure robotics names
watchlist.red_team: The outlook is positive, but the consensus beat may be limited, reducing the surprise factor.

watchlist.name: Harmonic Drive Systems
watchlist.why_today: Harmonic raised ordinary-profit guidance by 67%, with robot and semiconductor-equipment demand beating expectations. The setup fits the U.S. tech-led market tone.
watchpoints: Can it hold the 4,300-yen area and test higher
Does the market keep pricing the robot-demand recovery
Does the wider semiconductor-related tape remain supportive
falsifiers: Investors decide this is only a prior-year revision and wait for FY guidance
Theme buying fades and the stock turns into a gap-and-fade
watchlist.red_team: The revision is strong, but the stock remains highly sensitive to robotics-theme positioning.

watchlist.name: Kikkoman
watchlist.why_today: Kikkoman approved a buyback capped at 2.59% and 30 billion yen. In a high-oil, weak-yen environment, the capital-action support makes it worth monitoring despite cost risks.
watchpoints: Can it recover the 1,450-yen area
Does the buyback work as downside support
Can it absorb higher input-cost concerns
falsifiers: Raw-material and FX cost pressure overwhelms the buyback
Capital flows concentrate in semiconductors instead of defensives
watchlist.red_team: The buyback is clear, but higher oil and a weak yen still keep cost pressure in view.

watchlist.name: INPEX
watchlist.why_today: Brent touched the $107 area. Even if the index is bought on semiconductor strength, INPEX remains a useful monitor as a high-oil hedge.
watchpoints: Can Brent stay in the upper-$100 area during Tokyo hours
Do resource names keep relative strength even in a rising index
Does buying broaden to JAPEX and other upstream names
falsifiers: Oil reverses sharply and resource outperformance disappears
Capital concentrates so heavily in semis that resources lag
watchlist.red_team: It is a geopolitical hedge, so one peace headline can reverse the premise quickly.

watchlist.name: MIXI
watchlist.why_today: MIXI raised ordinary-profit guidance by 29%, materially reducing the expected profit decline. The key is whether growth rerating demand can follow.
watchpoints: Can it hold the 3,200-yen area after the open
Does the guidance raise drive a broader internet-services rerating
Does large-cap growth appetite continue
falsifiers: Investors focus on the fact that profit is still down year on year
Capital crowds into semiconductors and leaves other growth names behind
watchlist.red_team: The revision is positive, but the stock may still be judged against a year-on-year profit decline.

sources.title: ＭＩＸＩ(2121) 通期連結業績予想の修正に関するお知らせ

deep_dive.title: Techno Horizon: test whether the guidance raise and dividend hike can ride the AI-robotics tape

lines: The core setup is the combination of a 67% ordinary-profit guidance raise, a five-year record-profit target and a 10-yen dividend increase.
With U.S. chip stocks strong and AI-robotics themes active, this is the highest-priority opening check among small-cap catalysts.
The company disclosed the guidance and dividend revision at 16:00 JST on April 24, and Kabutan highlighted it as one of the top names in its April 27 morning focus list.
The broader market is watching the Nikkei's 60,000 level, but higher oil risk means clean earnings catalysts should still be favored.
Does the stock keep a strong bid after the open instead of turning into a gap-and-fade.
Does buying in robotics and physical-AI names spill over into Harmonic and FANUC as well.
Profit-taking dominates immediately after the opening print.
The dividend and record-profit angle is priced in too quickly and becomes a sell-the-news event.
Small-cap theme names can run ahead of volume, and a thinner tape can cause a fast reversal.
If U.S. tech futures fade after the open, AI-robotics buying can weaken across the group.

sources.title: Ｔホライゾン、前期経常を67％上方修正・5期ぶり最高益、配当も10円増額

no_trade_conditions: If the Nikkei fails to test 60,000 and quickly erases the futures-led opening premium
If Brent rises another leg and oil-cost pressure overwhelms semiconductor and domestic-demand buying
If USD/JPY moves toward 161 and intervention risk plus import-cost concerns expand sharply
If top catalyst names such as Techno Horizon and NRI cannot hold their opening premiums and the market becomes broadly gap-and-fade
